text: This is a video of a patient with left indirect inguinal hernia. In this video, I'll be showing the critical view of myopic tineal orifice as described by Dr. Diaz and Dr. Felix.

After the indirect sac was completely reduced, we typically have the nine steps of the critical view of the myopectenial orifice printed in the OR and we go through them one by one to make sure that we have it achieved prior to placing the mesh, which is step number nine. And it also helps us educate the residents.

The surgeon identifies and dissects the pubic tubercle across the midline and Cooper's ligament. For a large direct hernia, the dissection extends towards the contralateral Cooper's ligament. The surgeon then rolls out a direct hernia and removes any unusual fat in the Hesselbach's triangle.

The surgeon dissects at least two centimeters between the Cooper ligament and the bladder to facilitate the flat placement of the medial and inferior edge of the mesh towards the space of Retzius. Subsequently, dissection occurs between the Cooper ligament and the iliac vein to identify the femoral orifice and rule out a femoral hernia.

The surgeon dissects the indirect sac and peritoneum to parietalize the chord elements, ensuring they lie flat. The psoas muscle and iliac vessels are visualized. The sac and peritoneum are pulled upward carefully to prevent movement of the chord elements, while dissection continues between the chord elements to avoid missing any tail of the sac.
The surgeon identifies and reduces a cord lipoma, located lateral to the cord elements, ensuring it is not mistaken for lymph nodes.

The surgeon dissects the peritoneum lateral to the cord elements, extending the dissection laterally beyond the anterior superior iliac spine and sweeping it back inferiorly behind the inferior border of the mesh.
The surgeon prepares to place a self-fixating pro grip mesh, measuring sixteen by twelve centimeters, into the surgical field after completing steps one through eight.
The surgeon places a self-fixating mesh, ensuring it adapts to the contour of the surgical space and the quartz element without retaining undue memory.
